generator: curated-offline from MOE 6092/6091 syllabus PDFs; re-run npm run index:syllabus when PDFs are available

chunks.subject: Chemistry
chunks.title: Content structure — Sections I–III
chunks.content: Section I Matter – Structures and Properties: Experimental Chemistry; Particulate Nature of Matter; Chemical Bonding and Structure. Section II Chemical Reactions: Chemical Calculations; Acid-Base Chemistry; Qualitative Analysis; Redox Chemistry; Patterns in the Periodic Table; Chemical Energetics; Rate of Reactions. Section III Chemistry in a Sustainable World: Organic Chemistry; Maintaining Air Quality.

chunks.subject: Chemistry
chunks.title: 1. Experimental Chemistry
chunks.content: Candidates name apparatus for time, temperature, mass and volume (burettes, pipettes, measuring cylinders, gas syringes). Separation methods: filtration, crystallisation, distillation, fractional distillation, separating funnel, paper chromatography. Interpret chromatograms and Rf values. Deduce purity from melting and boiling point data.

chunks.subject: Chemistry
chunks.title: 2. The Particulate Nature of Matter
chunks.content: Describe solid, liquid and gaseous states and interconversion using kinetic particle theory. Explain diffusion of particles in liquids and gases. State effect of molecular mass and temperature on rate of diffusion. Atomic structure: protons, neutrons, electrons; proton number and nucleon number; isotopes; deduce subatomic particles in atoms and ions.

chunks.subject: Chemistry
chunks.title: 3. Chemical Bonding and Structure
chunks.content: Ionic bonding: electron transfer, dot-and-cross diagrams, giant ionic lattice, properties from lattice. Covalent bonding: shared pairs, simple molecules, properties from structure. Metallic bonding: positive ions in sea of electrons. Compare structures of diamond, graphite, silicon(IV) oxide, ionic and simple molecular substances to deduce properties.

chunks.subject: Chemistry
chunks.title: 4. Chemical Calculations
chunks.content: Write formulae and balanced equations including ionic equations. Mole concept: amount of substance, molar mass, Avogadro constant. Calculate reacting masses, volumes of gases at rtp, concentrations in mol/dm³. Titration calculations using n = cV. Empirical and molecular formulae from composition data.

chunks.subject: Chemistry
chunks.title: 5. Acid-Base Chemistry
chunks.content: Acids produce hydrogen ions in aqueous solution; alkalis produce hydroxide ions. Neutralisation forms salt and water. pH scale 0–14; universal indicator colours. Strong vs weak acids (degree of ionisation). Preparation of soluble salts by titration or excess solid; insoluble salts by precipitation.

chunks.subject: Chemistry
chunks.title: 6. Qualitative Analysis
chunks.content: Tests for anions: carbonate, chloride, iodide, nitrate, sulfate using specified reagents. Tests for cations: aluminium, ammonium, calcium, copper(II), iron(II), iron(III), zinc using aqueous sodium hydroxide and ammonia. Flame tests for Li, Na, K, Ca. Identify gases: ammonia, CO₂, chlorine, hydrogen, oxygen, SO₂.

chunks.subject: Chemistry
chunks.title: 7. Redox Chemistry
chunks.content: Oxidation is loss of electrons; reduction is gain of electrons (OIL RIG). Identify redox reactions by changes in oxidation state. Electrolysis of molten and aqueous compounds; products at electrodes. Electrochemical cells: simple cell voltage from reactivity series. Reactivity series and displacement reactions.

chunks.subject: Chemistry
chunks.title: 8. Patterns in the Periodic Table
chunks.content: Trends in Groups I, VII and noble gases. Group I: increasing reactivity down group, reaction with water. Group VII: halogen displacement, reactivity decreases down group. Transition elements: coloured compounds, catalytic behaviour. Period 3 oxides and hydroxides — acid/base character across period.

chunks.subject: Chemistry
chunks.title: 9–10. Energetics and Rate of Reactions
chunks.content: Exothermic and endothermic reactions; enthalpy change ΔH; energy profile diagrams with activation energy. Collision theory: frequency, orientation, energy. Factors affecting rate: temperature, concentration, pressure, surface area, catalyst. Interpret rate graphs and experimental methods for measuring rate.

chunks.subject: Chemistry
chunks.title: 11. Organic Chemistry
chunks.content: Homologous series: alkanes, alkenes, alcohols, carboxylic acids, esters. General formulae and functional groups. Combustion, addition reactions of alkenes, fermentation to ethanol, esterification. Polymers: addition polymerisation; structure of repeat unit. Naming and drawing structural formulae.

chunks.subject: Chemistry
chunks.title: 12. Maintaining Air Quality
chunks.content: Composition of clean dry air. Pollutants: CO, SO₂, NOₓ, particulates; sources and effects. Acid rain formation and prevention. Greenhouse effect and climate change link to CO₂ and other gases. Catalytic converters; flue gas desulfurisation. Singapore context for air quality management.

chunks.subject: Physics
chunks.title: Upper Secondary Physics — content sections
chunks.content: MOE Express Physics sections: Measurements; Newtonian Mechanics; Thermal Physics; Waves; Electricity and Magnetism; Radioactivity. Students develop inquiry practices, use models, relate physics to society and environment. Assessment includes practical work and data-based questions.

chunks.subject: Physics
chunks.title: Measurements
chunks.content: Physical quantities, SI units, prefixes. Measurement of length, time, mass using appropriate instruments. Precision, accuracy, parallax error. Scalars and vectors. Plot and interpret graphs including gradient and area under graph for physical meaning.

chunks.subject: Physics
chunks.title: Newtonian Mechanics
chunks.content: Kinematics: speed, velocity, acceleration, equations of motion, graphs. Dynamics: Newton's laws, free-body diagrams, friction. Mass, weight, density. Turning effect of forces: moment, centre of gravity, stability. Pressure in solids, liquids and gases; hydraulic systems. Energy, work, power; kinetic and gravitational potential energy; conservation of energy.

chunks.subject: Physics
chunks.title: Thermal Physics
chunks.content: Kinetic model of matter; Brownian motion evidence. Temperature and thermal equilibrium. Heat capacity Q = mcΔT; latent heat Q = mL. Conduction, convection, radiation. Practical applications: cooling, insulation, sea breeze. Changes of state on heating/cooling curves.

chunks.subject: Physics
chunks.title: Waves
chunks.content: Wave properties: amplitude, wavelength, frequency, period, speed v = fλ. Transverse and longitudinal waves; sound. Light: reflection, refraction, thin lens ray diagrams. Electromagnetic spectrum — order, properties, applications and safety. Sound: pitch, loudness, ultrasound uses.

chunks.subject: Physics
chunks.title: Electricity and Magnetism
chunks.content: Static electricity: charging, electric field, applications and hazards. Current, emf, potential difference; Ohm's law V = IR; I–V graphs for ohmic and non-ohmic conductors. Series and parallel circuits; combined resistance. Practical electricity: power P = IV, energy E = Pt, fuse and safety. Magnetism, electromagnetism, motor and generator principles, electromagnetic induction.

chunks.subject: Physics
chunks.title: Radioactivity
chunks.content: Background radiation. Types of radiation α, β, γ — nature, penetration, ionising power. Nuclear equations and half-life. Uses and hazards of radioactivity; safe handling and disposal. Medical and industrial applications; carbon dating overview.

chunks.subject: Science
chunks.title: Primary Science — themes
chunks.content: Primary Science themes: Diversity; Cycles; Systems; Interactions; Energy. Students observe, compare, classify; develop basic inquiry skills. Topics include plants, animals, human body, materials, forces, light, heat, electricity at appropriate primary levels.

chunks.subject: Science
chunks.sourceFile: syllabus/secondary/2021 Science Lower Secondary ExpressNormalAcademic Syllabus.pdf
chunks.title: Lower Secondary Science
chunks.content: Lower secondary integrated science builds foundations in scientific inquiry, measurement, and core ideas across biology, chemistry and physics before specialisation. Topics include cells, solutions, forces, energy forms, and matter, with emphasis on practical skills and ethics.
